D4 Colorimetry L4 Analyze Reference

ΔE76 — Straight-Line Colour Distance

move the sample patch through L*a*b* and read the plain Euclidean distance to the reference — the first colour-difference metric

The 1976 metric treats L*a*b* as ordinary space: the colour difference is just the straight-line distance between two points. Simple — but Lab is not perfectly uniform, so equal distances do not always look equal.

Drag the sample's lightness and a*, b* coordinates. The plot draws the line whose length is ΔE76. Roughly: ΔE ≈ 1 is the just-noticeable threshold, ΔE ≈ 2.3 a clearly distinct step.
